name: Honolulu Zoo Keiki Zoo
tagline: Walk right into the goat pen — mini goats, baby sheep, pigs, ponies, and alpaca.
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave or metered lots nearby
bestTime: Weekday mornings — animals are most active and crowds are thin
kamaaina: Kamaaina: $8/adult, $4/child (vs $19/$11 non-resident) with valid Hawaii ID
talkStory: The Honolulu Zoo dates to 1914, making it one of the oldest zoos west of the Mississippi. The Keiki Zoo section lets children walk right into enclosures with Nigerian dwarf goats, Barbados blackbelly sheep, and kunekune pigs. It is the only zoo in the U.S. established by a reigning monarch -- Queen Kapiolani donated the original land in Waikiki. Insider tip: the Twilight Tours on select Friday and Saturday evenings offer behind-the-scenes keeper access that daytime visitors never see.
